functions.name: accel
functions.description: Get a NumPy array of the acceleration of each gridpoint.

functions.name: disp
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the gridpoint displacement.

functions.name: extra
functions.signature: itasca.gridpointarray.extra(slot: int) -> array float{gridpoint} or float{gridpoint,3}
functions.description: Get the gridpoint extra data in the given slot as an array. Extra variables accessed by array must be of type float or vec.

functions.name: fill_disp
functions.description: Fill an existing array with the gridpoint displacement. The array must be the correct shape.

functions.name: fill_force_app
functions.description: Fill an existing array with the gridpoint applied force (including forces due to gravity). The array must be the correct shape.

functions.name: fill_force_load
functions.description: Fill an existing array with the gridpoint applied force. The array must be the correct shape.

functions.name: fill_force_unbal
functions.description: Fill an existing array with the gridpoint unbalanced force. The array must be the correct shape.

functions.name: fill_pos
functions.description: Fill an existing array with the gridpoint centroid location. The array must be the correct shape.

functions.name: fill_vel
functions.description: Fill an existing array with the gridpoint velocity. The array must be the correct shape.

functions.name: fixity
functions.description: Get a NumPy array of the gridpoint fixity condition.

functions.name: flow
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the unbalanced flow discharge at each gridpoint.

functions.name: fluid_load
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the additional fluid load at each gridpoint.

functions.name: fluid_modulus
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the fluid bulk modulus at each gridpoint.

functions.name: fluid_modulus_confined
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the fluid flow confined modulus times nodal volume at each gridpoint.

functions.name: fluid_tension
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the fluid tension at each gridpoint.

functions.name: fluid_unbal
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the fluid out-of-balance flow at each gridpoint: used in the fastflow solution scheme.

functions.name: fluid_vol_inv
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the inverse of the fluid nodal volume at each gridpoint.

functions.name: flux
functions.description: Get the unbalanced thermal flux at a each gridpoint as a NumPy array.

functions.name: force_app
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the gridpoint applied force. This includes all sources, including forces due to gravity.

functions.name: force_load
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the gridpoint applied force.

functions.name: force_unbal
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the gridpoint unbalanced force.

functions.name: ids
functions.description: Get the gridpoint ids as an array.

functions.name: in_group
functions.description: Return gridpoint group membership as a Boolean array.

functions.name: live_mechanical
functions.description: Return an array of True/False values for each gridpoint, a value of True indicates that a gridpoint is attached to at least one zone with an active mechanical model.

functions.name: porepressure
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the porepressure at each gridpoint.

functions.name: pos
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the gridpoint centroid location.

functions.name: set_disp
functions.description: Set the gridpoint displacement from an array.

functions.name: set_extra
functions.signature: itasca.gridpointarray.set_extra(slot: int, data: array float{gridpoint} or float{gridpoint,3}) -> None
functions.description: Set the gridpoint extra data in the given slot with an array. Extra variables set by array must be of type float or vec.

functions.name: set_fixity
functions.description: Set the gridpoint fixity condition.

functions.name: set_flow
functions.description: Set the unbalanced flow discharge at each gridpoint with a NumPy array.

functions.name: set_fluid_load
functions.description: Set the additional fluid load at each gridpoint with a NumPy array.

functions.name: set_fluid_modulus
functions.description: Set the fluid bulk modulus at each gridpoint with a NumPy array.

functions.name: set_fluid_tension
functions.description: Set the fluid tension at each gridpoint with a NumPy array.

functions.name: set_fluid_unbal
functions.description: Set the fluid out-of-balance flow at each gridpoin with a NumPy array.

functions.name: set_force_app
functions.description: Set the gridpoint applied force from an array.

functions.name: set_force_load
functions.description: Set the gridpoint applied force from an array.

functions.name: set_group
functions.signature: itasca.gridpointarray.set_group(membership: array bool{gridpoint}, group_name: str, slot=1) -> None
functions.description: Set gridpoint group from an array. Where membership True set the corresponding gridpoint to be a member of group group_name in the given slot.

functions.name: set_porepressure
functions.description: Set the pore pressure at each gridpoint with a NumPy array.

functions.name: set_pos
functions.description: Set the gridpoint centroid location from an array.

functions.name: set_temperature
functions.description: Set the temperature at each gridpoint with a NumPy array.

functions.name: set_vel
functions.description: Set the gridpoint velocity from an array.

functions.name: stiffness
functions.description: Get a NumPy array of the accumulated gridpoint effective stiffness.

functions.name: temperature
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the temperature at each gridpoint.

functions.name: vel
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the gridpoint velocity.

functions.name: zones
functions.signature: itasca.gridpointarray.zones() -> tuple of tuples of int
functions.description: Return the indices of the zones which each gridpoint is attached to. The indices are relative to the arrays returned by the zonearray interface functions. Note that the return type is not a numpy array in this case.
